description: THREE-WAY trigger discrimination for the word 'event': ANALYTICS behavioral events for measurement (this skill) vs the EXTERNAL API/webhook CONTRACT (api-event-architect) vs the INTERNAL streaming pipeline's transport events (streaming-event-architect); plus the schema-vs-instrumentation seam with product-analytics-instrumenter.

cases.prompt: Define our analytics tracking plan: consistent event names, typed properties with enums, the global properties every event carries, and how anonymous events stitch to identified users.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, three-way) — a tracking plan, event naming, property schema, and identity stitching are analytics measurement schema. No external contract and no pipeline transport is being designed.
cases.reason: Behavioral events captured to answer product questions (funnels, metrics, experiments) are this skill's core scope, distinct from an integration contract and from pipeline transport.

cases.prompt: Design our public webhook and event-feed contract for partners: a versioned signed envelope, tenant-scoped subscriptions, at-least-once delivery with retries, and a deprecation policy.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (three-way) — signed versioned envelopes, subscriptions, and delivery guarantees are the external contract offered to integrators, a promise, not a measurement schema.
cases.reason: api-event-architect owns the external event contract. Analytics events have no external consumers to make promises to; they exist to be measured.

cases.prompt: Design the internal streaming backbone that moves events between our services: topics, partition keys, delivery semantics, dead-letter queues, and replay.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (three-way) — topics, partitions, delivery semantics, DLQ, and replay are the internal transport pipeline, not analytics meaning and not an external contract.
cases.reason: streaming-event-architect owns the internal data-pipeline transport. This skill defines what an analytics event MEANS and its properties, regardless of the pipe it may ride.

cases.prompt: We have the tracking plan. Now decide where in the code each event fires, client vs server, how consent gates capture, and how we QA that events fire correctly.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — firing points, client-vs-server, consent gating, and tracking QA are instrumentation. The schema they implement against is defined here but not what's asked.
cases.reason: product-analytics-instrumenter owns where/how events fire. This skill owns the schema (what events and properties exist).
